steps.expected: Stable JSON object with success, data, error, meta keys
steps.observed: JSON output with success=true, data containing commands/name/description, error=null, meta with command=help and mode=json
Identical output structure on second run

evidence.notes: All required top-level keys present: success, data, error, meta. meta.command identifies the help contract.

steps.observed: JSON output with success=true, data.name=impartus, data.version=dev, meta.command=version

evidence.notes: Both orderings produce identical machine-readable envelope semantics

assertions.title: Unknown commands fail predictably in JSON mode

steps.expected: JSON error envelope with success=false, data=null
steps.observed: JSON output with success=false, data=null, error.message='unknown command: nope', meta.command=nope

evidence.notes: Deterministic error text that agents can branch on without parsing help output

assertions.title: JSON mode never falls back to interactive prompts

steps.action: Run ./impartus lectures --json with empty stdin and 3s timeout
steps.observed: JSON error envelope with message 'lectures requires --subject/-s and --session/-S', exit code 1

steps.action: Run ./impartus download --json with empty stdin and 3s timeout
steps.observed: JSON error envelope with message 'download requires --subject/-s and --session/-S', exit code 1

evidence.notes: Both commands returned structured JSON errors immediately without waiting for stdin input

steps.observed: JSON output with success=true, data.baseURL, data.health, data.port=8080, data.status=ready, data.note explaining non-blocking behavior. Exit code 0.
Command exited immediately with exit code 0, no foreground blocking

evidence.processCheck: Command exited immediately, no blocking server process started

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. CLI JSON envelope contract is fully implemented and working as specified.
